sn22.131:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Sāriputta and Ven. Mahā Koṭṭhita were staying near Vārāṇasī in the Deer Park at Isipatana. As he was sitting to one side, Ven. Mahā Koṭṭhita said to Ven. Sāriputta,
sn22.131:1.2: “‘Ignorance, ignorance,’ it is said, friend Sāriputta.
sn22.131:1.4: And to what extent is one immersed in ignorance?”
sn22.131:2.1: “There is the case, my friend, where an uninstructed, run-of-the-mill person doesn’t discern, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and the escape from—form.
sn22.131:2.2: “He doesn’t discern, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and the escape from—feeling.
sn22.131:2.3: “He doesn’t discern, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and the escape from—perception.
sn22.131:2.4: “He doesn’t discern, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and the escape from—fabrications.
sn22.131:2.5: “He doesn’t discern, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and the escape from—consciousness.
sn22.131:2.7: and it’s to this extent that one is immersed in ignorance.”
